task_completion.description: Update pom.xml with JUnit 5 and Mockito dependencies
task_completion.visual_evidence: The pom.xml file is visible in an editor, containing <dependency> entries for 'junit-jupiter' and 'mockito-core' (versions 5.x).

task_completion.description: Create the PaymentServiceTest class in the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named PaymentServiceTest.java is visible in the file explorer or open in an editor, located in the src/test/java/com/payment/ directory.

task_completion.description: Mock dependencies and inject them into PaymentService
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PaymentServiceTest.java file shows the CreditCardProcessor and TransactionRepository being mocked (e.g., using @Mock annotations or Mockito.mock() calls) and injected into the PaymentService instance.

task_completion.description: Implement test for a successful charge
task_completion.visual_evidence: A test method in PaymentServiceTest.java is visible that configures a successful charge, asserts a true return value, and verifies a SUCCESS transaction status is saved.

task_completion.description: Implement test for a declined charge
task_completion.visual_evidence: A test method in PaymentServiceTest.java is visible that configures a declined charge, asserts a false return value, and verifies a DECLINED transaction status is saved.

task_completion.description: Implement test for a network exception
task_completion.visual_evidence: A test method in PaymentServiceTest.java is visible that configures a network exception (e.g., using thenThrow), asserts a false return value, and verifies an ERROR transaction status is saved.

task_completion.description: Verify all interactions with the mocks
task_completion.visual_evidence: The test methods in PaymentServiceTest.java contain Mockito.verify() calls for both the CreditCardProcessor (e.g., verify(processor).charge(...)) and the TransactionRepository (e.g., verify(repository).save(...)).

integrity.description: The agent used an IDE or text editor to write the code
integrity.visual_evidence: The Java files and pom.xml are seen open in an IDE (like IntelliJ) or a text editor (like VS Code, vim, nano), rather than being created solely via terminal commands like 'echo'.

integrity.description: The test class contains valid Java code
integrity.visual_evidence: The test class contains valid Java syntax with proper JUnit 5 (@Test) and Mockito imports, not just pseudocode or plain text.

integrity.description: The main PaymentService class was not modified to bypass tests
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence that the agent modified the original PaymentService.java file to make the tests pass artificially.
